summary: Validated Vercel deployment config fix: both vercel.json files are valid JSON, contain framework=create-react-app, installCommand uses yarn (not npm), buildCommand=yarn build, outputDirectory=build, and neither contains 'cd frontend' (the prior root cause). yarn.lock exists at /app/frontend, package-lock.json does NOT exist (so Vercel will auto-detect yarn). package.json has react/react-dom ^19.2.7 and resolutions glob=10.4.5, rimraf=5.0.10. Local smoke test of the exact command Vercel will execute ('yarn install --frozen-lockfile && yarn build' in /app/frontend) succeeded — produced /app/frontend/build with index.html and static/{js,css}/. Backend /api/health returns {status:ok}. Public frontend URL returns HTTP 200.
passed: Both vercel.json use framework=create-react-app, installCommand=yarn install --frozen-lockfile, buildCommand=yarn build, outputDirectory=build
/app/frontend/package-lock.json does NOT exist (Vercel will pick yarn)
yarn build succeeds in /app/frontend and produces build/index.html + build/static/{js,css}
action_items: No fixes needed. User must still replace 'YOUR-RENDER-BACKEND.onrender.com' placeholders in vercel.json rewrites with their actual Render backend URL post-deploy (intentional per problem statement).

Minor (non-blocking): Vercel build log will show several yarn 'Resolution field incompatible with requested version' warnings (e.g. glob, rimraf, nth-check). These are expected from the resolutions block forcing newer versions and do not fail the build.
